ahmedabad.lede: Ahmedabad was doing serious science long before most Indian tech hubs existed. Vikram Sarabhai started the Physical Research Laboratory here in 1947, and ISRO's Space Applications Centre still builds the imaging payloads for missions like Chandrayaan and the Mars Orbiter from Ambawadi Vistar. Gujarat Technological University, which every engineering college in the state answers to, is headquartered at Chandkheda. For a child in an Ahmedabad school, GUJCET and JEE Main are the doors, and ACPC runs the counselling behind them.

faqs.q: Do you offer coding classes for GSEB students in Ahmedabad?
faqs.a: Yes. Ahmedabad schools run GSEB alongside CBSE, ICSE, IB and Cambridge, and our classes sit beside all of them rather than replacing school work. We teach the programming itself — Python, Java, web development — so the computer paper stops being something your child only reads about.

faqs.q: Can students from GTU-affiliated colleges or Gujarat University join?
faqs.a: Yes. Our college track is built for exactly that. Students at GTU-affiliated engineering colleges and Gujarat University join for real Python, Java, full-stack and AI/ML work aimed at internships and placements, not another certificate.

ahmedabad._excluded: DA-IICT, GIFT City, IIT Gandhinagar are in GANDHINAGAR not Ahmedabad — my own prompt suggested DA-IICT and the researcher correctly refused it. GSEB is headquartered in Gandhinagar too, so never imply the board sits in Ahmedabad. GCET (MBA/MCA, GTU) is a DIFFERENT exam from GUJCET (engineering, GSEB) — do not conflate. GUJCET weightage figures conflict across sources, so no weightage number is claimed.

pune.lede: Pune has been teaching engineers since 1854, when COEP opened its doors, and it is still where a good deal of India's computing actually gets made. C-DAC is headquartered out at Pashan, and it was there that India built the PARAM supercomputer after the United States refused to sell it one. The board that sets your child's SSC and HSC papers is headquartered in Pune too — not in Mumbai. From there the doors onward are MHT-CET and JEE Main.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to Maharashtra State Board students in Pune?
faqs.a: Yes — and it is worth knowing that the board setting those SSC and HSC papers is headquartered in Pune itself. We do not coach the school syllabus back at your child. We teach the programming underneath it, so the computer paper stops being a memory test.

faqs.q: Can SPPU or COEP students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. COEP has been turning out engineers since 1854 and Savitribai Phule Pune University since 1949, but a degree still does not hand a fresher a portfolio. The college track is built for that gap: real Python, Java, full-stack and AI/ML projects you can open in a Hinjawadi interview.

pune._excluded: Fergusson 1885 is Wikipedia-only — the college's own site supports 1884 (society) and 1895 (campus), so no Fergusson year is used. Hinjawadi headcount omitted: sources conflict (3 lakh vs 4.5 lakh). COEP's 'third oldest in Asia' is COEP's own claim, so not asserted. 'Oxford of the East' is a nickname, not a fact. IGCSE presence in Pune unverified, so not claimed.

mumbai.lede: Mumbai has had a university since 1857 — one of the first three in India — and an IIT since 1958. The Powai campus was built with help no other IIT received in quite the same form: UNESCO and the Soviet government supplied the equipment, the experts and the fellowships. TIFR has done fundamental science from Colaba since 1945, and VJTI has taught engineering since 1887. Your child's route into any of it runs through MHT-CET, or JEE Main and Advanced.

faqs.q: Which school board do you follow for Mumbai students?
faqs.a: None of them, deliberately. Mumbai runs the Maharashtra State Board, CBSE, ICSE, IB and Cambridge IGCSE side by side, and a coding class built around one syllabus is useless to the other four. We teach the language itself and fit around whichever board your child actually sits.

faqs.q: Can IIT Bombay or Mumbai University students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. The college track takes students from IIT Bombay, Mumbai University, VJTI and the engineering colleges around the city who want working Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML projects to show, rather than another line on a CV.

mumbai._excluded: VJTI 'one of Asia's oldest' — MY prompt suggested it; VJTI's own About page claims only the 1887 founding, so only the year is used. SEEPZ deliberately NOT called a software/IT hub: the official SEEPZ page says electronics (and gems/jewellery from 1987-88), never software. ICT Mumbai (1933) omitted — its site 403s every fetch and the year could not be opened at source. TIFR's move from Bangalore not asserted (only the 1945 founding and the Colaba address are verified). No school counts or rankings anywhere.

bengaluru.lede: India's space programme is run from Bengaluru — ISRO's headquarters sit at Antariksh Bhavan on New BEL Road — and the city has done science since IISc opened in 1909 on a Tata endowment. What matters more for your child is how Karnataka counts. KCET builds its engineering merit list from the entrance test and 2nd PUC marks in the same subjects, taken, in the statutory wording, "in equal proportions". Board marks here carry weight they simply do not carry at JEE Main.

faqs.q: Do Karnataka PUC board marks matter for engineering admission?
faqs.a: More than most parents expect. Under Rule 4 of the CET-2006 Admission Rules, KCET merit is built from Physics, Chemistry and Maths in the entrance test and the same subjects at 2nd PUC, taken in equal proportions. At JEE Main, board marks only set an eligibility bar. That difference is worth knowing before Class 11, not after it.

faqs.q: Can IIIT-B or UVCE students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. UVCE has taught engineering here since 1917 and IIIT-B was built in 1998 with the IT industry's own money, but a syllabus is still not a portfolio. Our college track is project work: Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML, built until it actually runs.

bengaluru._excluded: Aggregators say KCET is '50:50' but they are coaching sites; the statutory wording is 'in equal proportions' (Rule 4, CET-2006), quoted from the KEA brochure. IISc 'oldest research institute' NOT claimed - IISc's own pages never say it; the verified 1909 date does the same work. UVCE's 'fifth engineering college in the country' is its own self-claim. RVCE dropped (site TLS-failed; 1963 unverifiable at source). The KCET rule is Karnataka-wide, not Bengaluru-only.

hyderabad.lede: India's first technological university was founded here: JNTU opened on 2 October 1972. HITEC City followed on 22 November 1998, the same year IIIT Hyderabad was set up as the country's first not-for-profit public-private institute of its kind. Osmania has been teaching since 1918. Your child's route onward is TG EAPCET or JEE Main — and if they aim at IIIT-H itself, its own UGEE, which tests research aptitude alongside subject knowledge.

faqs.q: Is it TS EAMCET or TG EAPCET now?
faqs.a: Officially TG EAPCET. Telangana separated from Andhra Pradesh in 2014 and the state code later moved from TS to TG, so the exam most people still call TS EAMCET is now the Telangana Engineering, Agriculture and Pharmacy Common Entrance Test, conducted by JNTU Hyderabad. Both names point at the same exam. Telangana also runs two school boards: BSE Telangana for Class 10, and TGBIE separately for Intermediate.

faqs.q: Can IIIT Hyderabad or JNTUH students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. Students from IIIT-H, JNTUH, Osmania, the University of Hyderabad and BITS Hyderabad take the college track. The point of it is a deployed project rather than a syllabus — Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML, built until someone else can use it.

hyderabad._excluded: IIT HYDERABAD IS NOT IN HYDERABAD - its campus is at Kandi, Sangareddy district. Never name it as a Hyderabad institution. T-Hub's 'world's largest startup hub' is self-description. Microsoft IDC 'largest outside Redmond' is Wikipedia-only. There is NO CBSE regional office in Hyderabad - CBSE's own document shows the region served from Kakinada - despite search engines asserting one.

chennai.lede: Tamil Nadu is the one big state where an engineering seat turns on school marks instead of a coaching-driven entrance exam. TNEA builds its merit list from Class 12 marks alone, reduced to 200 — Maths 100, Physics 50, Chemistry 50 — and normalises every other board against the Tamil Nadu State Board. Chennai has taught engineering since 1794, when the College of Engineering, Guindy began life as a School of Survey with eight students near Fort St. George.

faqs.q: Does Tamil Nadu need an entrance exam for engineering?
faqs.a: Not for B.E. or B.Tech. TNEA is single-window counselling and the merit list comes from Class 12 marks alone, cut to 200 across Maths, Physics and Chemistry, with CBSE and ICSE marks normalised against the Tamil Nadu State Board. One caveat parents miss: this does not extend to B.Arch, which is still ranked on NATA or JEE Paper 2.

faqs.q: Can IIT Madras or Anna University students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. Anna University's CEG campus traces back to 1794 and IIT Madras to 1959, and both teach theory well. What the college track adds is the other half — Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML, written until it deploys.

chennai._excluded: TNEA marks-only applies to B.E./B.Tech ONLY - B.Arch ranks on 400 marks incl. NATA/JEE Paper 2. The researcher nearly published the B.Arch rule as the engineering rule, so the FAQ states the caveat explicitly. CEG's 'oldest technical institution in Asia' is CEG's OWN claim - not asserted in our voice. NIRF ranking omitted: it would need re-dating every September. OMR/TIDEL Park dropped - tidelpark.com carries no founding date and its history pages 404.

jaipur.lede: Jaipur has been building computing machines since 1734. The Jantar Mantar's Vrihat Samrat Yantra is an 88-foot stone sundial, its face angled to 27 degrees to match the city's own latitude, and it still tells the time to roughly two seconds. That is what an algorithm looked like before there were computers to run one on. The board that sets your child's papers, RBSE, was founded here in 1957 before moving to Ajmer; the routes onward are REAP and JEE Main.

faqs.q: Do you offer coding classes for RBSE students in Jaipur?
faqs.a: Yes. Jaipur schools run RBSE, CBSE, ICSE and IB, and our classes work alongside any of them. One thing worth knowing: RBSE was founded in Jaipur in 1957 but has been headquartered in Ajmer since 1961, so the board setting your child's papers is not actually a Jaipur body.

faqs.q: Can MNIT Jaipur or LNMIIT students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. Students from MNIT Jaipur, LNMIIT, the University of Rajasthan and Manipal Jaipur take the college track. It is project work rather than lectures: Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML, built to the point where you can hand someone a URL.

jaipur._excluded: RBSE IS HEADQUARTERED IN AJMER, not Jaipur - it was only founded in Jaipur (1957) and moved in 1961. Mahindra World City 'largest planned IT SEZ in India' is press-release copy absent from Mahindra's own page. BSDU 'India's first skill university' is its own marketing. Manipal Jaipur's campus is ~30km out at Dehmi Kalan, so it is never described as 'in Jaipur'.

indore.lede: Indore is one of the few places where a fifteen-year-old can already see the whole ladder. IIM Indore launched India's first five-year Integrated Programme in Management in 2011 — a degree you enter straight after Class XII — and SGSITS has taught engineering here since 1952. IIT Indore arrived in 2009, though its campus sits out at Simrol on Khandwa Road rather than in the city itself. The routes are JEE, MP DTE counselling, or IPMAT.

faqs.q: Can a Class 12 student in Indore apply straight to IIM Indore?
faqs.a: Yes, through IPMAT. IIM Indore launched its five-year Integrated Programme in Management in 2011 and calls it the first of its kind in India: students enter directly after Class XII. It is an aptitude test rather than a syllabus test, and aptitude is built over years rather than crammed in one.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to MP Board students in Indore?
faqs.a: Yes. Indore schools run MPBSE, CBSE, ICSE, Cambridge and IB between them, so the classes are built to be board-agnostic. Your child learns Python or Java properly, and the school computer paper takes care of itself.

indore._excluded: MY PROMPT WAS WRONG: 'Indore is notable for having both an IIT and an IIM' is NOT distinctive - Mumbai has had both since NITIE became IIM Mumbai in Aug 2023 (PIB), and Jammu likely too. No uniqueness is claimed anywhere. IIT Indore's campus is at Simrol ~25km outside the city, stated honestly rather than implied to be in it. Daly College is NOT ICSE (aggregators say it is; the school's own site shows CBSE + Cambridge only).

delhi.lede: Delhi keeps 85 per cent of the seats at its own engineering institutions — NSUT, DTU, IIIT-Delhi, IGDTUW and DSEU — for candidates who passed Class 12 from a school inside the NCT. Your child is competing mainly against other Delhi students for them, and the allocation runs on JEE Main. IIIT-Delhi even adds bonus points for Olympiads and programming contests. DTU has been teaching here since 1941, when it opened as Delhi Polytechnic.

faqs.q: Does a Delhi address help with admission to DTU or NSUT?
faqs.a: Substantially. 85 per cent of seats at NSUT, DTU, IIIT-Delhi, IGDTUW and DSEU are reserved for Delhi Region candidates — defined as having passed the qualifying exam from an institution inside the NCT of Delhi — leaving 15 per cent for everyone else. IIIT-Delhi also awards bonus points for Olympiads and programming contests, which is the rare case where competitive coding converts directly into a seat.

faqs.q: Which board do Delhi schools follow?
faqs.a: Overwhelmingly CBSE, which is itself headquartered in Dwarka. Delhi set up its own board, DBSE, in 2021, but it has been wound up from 2025 and its Schools of Specialised Excellence moved back to CBSE. ICSE, IB and Cambridge IGCSE schools also operate in the city, and our classes run alongside any of them.

delhi._excluded: MY PROMPT WAS WRONG: I described DBSE as 'the newer Delhi Board of School Education (2021)'. It is DEFUNCT - wound up from 2025, its schools moved to CBSE, confirmed by an official DoE circular AND the Education Minister on record. Calling DBSE a live Delhi board would have been a false fact. No 'X% of Delhi schools are CBSE' claim: no board-wise count was verifiable. NIOS omitted (HQ is in Noida, UP).

lucknow.lede: The largest school on earth is in Lucknow. City Montessori School started in 1959 with five pupils in rented rooms and now holds a Guinness World Record with 61,345 pupils across 21 campuses in this city. Down the road, La Martiniere — open since 1845 — is the only school anywhere to hold royal battle honours, for the defence of the Residency in 1857. Lucknow takes schooling unusually seriously. The routes onward are JEE Main through UPTAC, or JEECUP for polytechnics.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to UP Board students in Lucknow?
faqs.a: Yes. Lucknow runs the UP Board alongside CBSE, ICSE and IB, and our classes sit beside whichever your child attends. The UP Board describes itself as the biggest examining body in the world; whatever the ranking, its Class 10 and 12 papers are not where a child learns to actually write software. That is the gap we fill.

faqs.q: Can IIM Lucknow or AKTU students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. AKTU affiliates the engineering colleges across the whole state from its Jankipuram campus, and its B.Tech admissions run on JEE Main through UPTAC. Students already inside that system take our college track for the part it does not cover: Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML built into something that runs.

lucknow._excluded: 'UP Board is India's largest board' is UPMSP's OWN self-description and is NOT Lucknow-specific (equally true of Kanpur/Varanasi/Agra) - so it is attributed in-sentence ('describes itself as') rather than asserted. No school counts: UPMSP says 22,000+ recognised schools, Wikipedia says 29,208 - do not publish a number. No AKTU affiliated-college count: official says 756, Wikipedia ~800. CMS's 2002 UNESCO peace prize omitted (UNESCO's own doc 403s). La Martiniere battle honours rests on Wikipedia; the school's own site 403s - high confidence, not primary.

kolkata.lede: Indian computing started in this city. The Indian Statistical Institute took delivery of the HEC-2M in February 1956 — by ISI's own account the first electronic computer installed in India, and ISI was the first place in the country to turn out trained programmers. Ten years later ISI and Jadavpur University built ISIJU-1 together, the first solid state computer made in India, commissioned in April 1966. Kolkata has been teaching people to program for longer than anywhere else in the country.

faqs.q: Is ISC Computer Science in Kolkata taught in Java?
faqs.a: Yes. CISCE prescribes Java for ISC Class 12 Computer Science — version 5.0 or later, with BlueJ strongly recommended — and the practical exam asks candidates to hand-write a program in a planning session before keying it in and running it. That matters more here than elsewhere: West Bengal is one of the few states with more ICSE and ISC schools than CBSE ones, 460 against 418 on the state's own 2023 count. We teach Java properly for exactly this reason.

faqs.q: Can Jadavpur, Calcutta University or ISI students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. Jadavpur has taught engineering since 1955, Calcutta University since 1857, and ISI has taught statistics and computer science since 1931 — the theory is not the gap. The college track is for the other half: Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML carried through to something that runs and can be shown.

kolkata._excluded: IIT KHARAGPUR IS NOT IN KOLKATA (~120km away) - never named. The 460-vs-418 figures are CISCE vs CBSE ONLY: the same notification's WBBSE figure (740) counts UNAIDED schools only and must never be compared against the other two. No Kolkata city-level school count exists in the source, so none is claimed; aggregator ICSE counts (353/375) conflict and were discarded. The ISC-Java syllabus is CISCE-wide/national - it is distinctive for Kolkata only IN COMBINATION with the state's atypical ICSE density, and the copy says exactly that rather than implying a Kolkata-only syllabus. IIEST 'second-oldest engineering college' dropped (its own site says second, Wikipedia says third). Salt Lake Sector V dropped: an IT hub is not city-unique and the '430 acres' figure is Wikipedia-only. ISI's live history page 500s and Calcutta University blocks fetching, so both are cited to Wayback snapshots of the institutions' OWN pages, opened and read.
kolkata._note: Dropped a drafted line placing the child 'about seven kilometres from where the country learned to': the classes are online from the child's home, which can be anywhere in Kolkata, so the distance was both unverifiable and incoherent.

patna.lede: Patna has taught engineering since 1886, when what is now NIT Patna opened as the Bihar School of Engineering — and Patna College goes back to 1863, before the university that was later built on it. One thing surprises parents: Bihar runs no state engineering entrance at all. UGEAC fills the seats in the state's government engineering colleges on JEE Main rank. BCECE, the exam everyone assumes is the engineering route, now covers pharmacy, medical and agriculture.

faqs.q: Which entrance exam does a Bihar student need for engineering?
faqs.a: JEE Main. Bihar does not run a separate state engineering entrance: UGEAC, conducted by BCECEB, allots seats in the state's government engineering colleges on JEE Main rank, and B.Arch uses JEE Main plus NATA. BCECE itself now covers pharmacy, medical and agriculture, not engineering — a distinction a lot of sites still get wrong.

faqs.q: Can IIT Patna or NIT Patna students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes, and one practical note: IIT Patna's campus is at Bihta, about 40 km out, while NIT Patna sits on Ashok Rajpath in the city. Since our classes are live online, neither commute matters. The college track is Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML built into something that actually runs.

patna._excluded: BCECE IS NOT BIHAR'S ENGINEERING ENTRANCE - the BCECEB portal states UGEAC runs on JEE Main. Most sites get this wrong. Super 30 omitted from the copy: super30.org confirms the 30-student intake and the free coaching/lodging/food, but not the 2002 founding or Anand Kumar's name, which are Wikipedia-only. No JEE candidate-count statistics for Bihar - no official state-wise breakdown was verifiable.

kochi.lede: Kerala reads. At the 2011 Census the state recorded 93.91 per cent literacy against a national figure around 73 — the highest of any Indian state, on the State Planning Board's own numbers. Kochi turned that into an industry: Infopark opened in 2004 and now runs to 323 acres and 9.2 million square feet, with more than 580 companies and over 70,000 IT professionals. CUSAT has been here since 1971. The route in is KEAM, through the state's centralised allotment.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to Kerala state board students in Kochi?
faqs.a: Yes. Kerala splits its state board in two — Pareeksha Bhavan runs the SSLC for Class 10, and the Directorate of Higher Secondary Education runs Plus One and Plus Two separately — and CBSE and ICSE schools operate here as well. Our classes are built to work beside any of them.

faqs.q: Can CUSAT or Model Engineering College students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. Infopark is on the doorstep with 580-odd companies in it, which is exactly why a portfolio matters more here than a transcript. The college track is project work: Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML, carried through to something you can deploy and link to.

kochi._excluded: REFUSED: 'Kerala declared fully literate in 2024'. Under ULLAS/NILP the bar is 95% adult literacy, Mizoram was declared before Kerala, and Kerala had not been declared under that framework. The widely-quoted 96.2% is a different survey, not the Census. Only the Census 2011 figure (93.91%) is used, attributed to the State Planning Board. CUSAT 1971 and MEC 1989 are second-source (cusat.ac.in TLS-failed, ihrd.ac.in 403) so no founding year is asserted for MEC. Several Kochi schools assumed to be ICSE are actually CBSE (Choice School, Toc H, Delta Study).

visakhapatnam.lede: Andhra University has taught here since 1926, and the Eastern Naval Command runs the Indian Navy's eastern seaboard from this city. Vizag also sits on the far side of a split most families still trip over: Andhra Pradesh and Telangana have run separate examination boards since 2014, so a Vizag student sits the SSC under the Board of Secondary Education Andhra Pradesh, with Intermediate under a different body again. The engineering route is AP EAPCET, or JEE Main.

faqs.q: Which board and entrance exam apply to a Visakhapatnam student?
faqs.a: Andhra Pradesh ones, not Telangana's. Since the 2014 bifurcation the two states run separate examination bodies: Vizag students sit the Class 10 SSC under BSEAP and Classes 11-12 Intermediate under BIEAP. For engineering the route is AP EAPCET, conducted by JNTU Kakinada for APSCHE, or JEE Main for the national institutes.

faqs.q: Can Andhra University or GITAM students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. Andhra University's engineering college has been running since the 1950s and IIM Visakhapatnam arrived in 2015, so the theory is well covered locally. The college track adds the part that is not: Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML written until it deploys.

visakhapatnam._excluded: AUCE 1955 and GITAM 1980 are second-source (gitam.edu 403s; AU's own page lists the engineering college without a year), so the copy says 'since the 1950s' rather than asserting 1955. AUCE's 'first Indian Chemical Engineering department' claim dropped - uncited even on Wikipedia.

surat.lede: Surat cuts and polishes 42 per cent of the world's rough diamonds — the city government's own figure, not the inflated one that circulates — and makes 40 per cent of India's man-made fabric. It is a city that builds things at scale, and it has trained the engineers to do it since 1961, when SVNIT opened. For your child the route runs through GUJCET, where the merit list is half board marks in Maths, Physics and Chemistry and half the entrance percentile.

faqs.q: How much do Gujarat board marks count for engineering admission?
faqs.a: Half of it. ACPC builds the merit list from 50 per cent board theory percentile in Maths, Physics and Chemistry and 50 per cent GUJCET percentile, for 95 per cent of seats in government and grant-in-aid institutions. Several coaching sites report a 60:40 split; the official ACPC admission programme says 50:50.

faqs.q: Can SVNIT or VNSGU students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. SVNIT has been an engineering school since 1961 and Veer Narmad South Gujarat University covers seven districts from its Surat campus. Neither of them ships you a portfolio at graduation. That is the college track: Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML, built until it runs.

surat._excluded: REFUSED: the famous '90% of the world's diamonds'. Surat Municipal Corporation's OWN Contribution page says 42% of world rough cutting/polishing (70% of the nation's). The 90% figure is everywhere in secondary press with no primary backing. Also refused: the 60:40 ACPC weightage reported by aggregators - the official PDF says 50:50. SMC does not date its figures, so they are undated official claims.

vadodara.lede: Vadodara has been teaching technology since 1890, when Maharaja Sayajirao Gaekwad III started the Kala Bhavan Technical Institute — an idea first floated at a prize-giving at Baroda College in 1886. It became MSU's Faculty of Technology and Engineering in 1949. The city stayed an engineering town: the district administration reckons over 35 per cent of India's power transmission and distribution equipment makers are here, with some 800 ancillaries around them. The route in is GUJCET and ACPC.

faqs.q: Do you offer coding classes for GSEB students in Vadodara?
faqs.a: Yes. Vadodara runs GSEB, CBSE, IB and Cambridge IGCSE across its schools, and our classes work alongside whichever your child attends. What we teach is the programming itself — Python, Java, web development — rather than a second pass over the school computer paper.

faqs.q: Can MSU Baroda students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. MSU's Faculty of Technology has roots going back to Kala Bhavan in 1890, which is a long lineage and still not a portfolio. The college track is where the projects come from: Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML, built to the point of being usable.

vadodara._excluded: MSU's 1949/1881 founding is Wikipedia-only (msubaroda.ac.in TLS-failed), so the copy leans on the Kala Bhavan dates, which come from an official .gov.in page. REFUSED: 'MSU is the only university in Gujarat with English medium' - not confirmed. Parul University omitted: its main campus is in Waghodia taluka, Vadodara DISTRICT, not the city.

noida.lede: The board that lets anyone in India finish school from anywhere is headquartered in Noida: the National Institute of Open Schooling has run from Sector 62 since 1989. Sector 16-A holds Film City, roughly 100 acres and 16 studios, from which the government's own directory counts 350 channels broadcasting to 162 countries. Noida is in Uttar Pradesh, so the state board is UPMSP — not Haryana's, whatever the NCR postcode suggests. The routes onward are JEE Main and CUET-UG.

faqs.q: Is Noida under the UP board or a Delhi board?
faqs.a: Uttar Pradesh. Noida sits in Gautam Buddha Nagar district, so the state board is UPMSP, the same one that examines Lucknow and Kanpur — not a Delhi board and not Haryana's, despite Noida and Gurgaon both being NCR. In practice most Noida schools are CBSE, and ICSE, IB and Cambridge IGCSE schools operate here too.

faqs.q: Can Amity or JIIT students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. JIIT sits in Sector 62 and Amity has been a Noida university since 2005. The college track is for the gap neither closes on its own: Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML built into a project you can hand someone.

noida._excluded: REFUSED: NTA (which runs JEE/NEET/CUET) headquartered in Noida - would have been a perfect hook, but sources conflict; Wikipedia puts its HQ in Okhla, New Delhi, and the Sector 62 address appears to be an IITK Outreach Centre. Greater Noida institutions (Shiv Nadar, Sharda, Bennett, GBU) excluded - different urban centre. No board-share percentage: no source quantifies CBSE share in Noida. Amity campus size omitted (aggregators say 1,300 acres, Wikipedia says 60).

gurgaon.lede: Gurgaon is in Haryana, so its state board is BSEH at Bhiwani — a different state and a different board from Noida's, whatever the shared NCR postcode suggests. The city grew up around Maruti Suzuki in the early 1980s and kept going: the district administration calls the Gurugram-Manesar-Bawal stretch the auto hub of the country, and DLF Cyber City packs roughly 15 million square feet of offices into about 125 acres, much of it Global Capability Centres. MDI has been here since 1973.

faqs.q: Which school board applies in Gurgaon, and does it differ from Noida?
faqs.a: It differs, and the confusion is common. Gurgaon is in Haryana, so the state board is the Board of School Education Haryana at Bhiwani. Noida is in Uttar Pradesh under UPMSP. Both sit in the NCR and both are heavily CBSE in practice, with ICSE, IB and Cambridge IGCSE schools present, but the state machinery behind them is not the same.

faqs.q: Can Gurugram University or MDI students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. Cyber City is full of Global Capability Centres hiring engineers a few kilometres away, and they interview on what you have built. The college track exists for that: Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML, taken as far as a working deployment.

gurgaon._excluded: FLAGGED AND NOT USED: gurugram.gov.in claims the city has 'the 3rd highest per capita income in India'. It is on an official page but carries no date, citation or dataset, and it is exactly the kind of stat that propagates unchecked. No board-share percentage: the two CBSE counts found (156 vs 117 for the same district) came from aggregators and contradicted each other.

nagpur.lede: Nagpur has taught at university level since 1923, when RTMNU opened with six affiliated colleges and 927 students, and VNIT has trained engineers here since 1960. The city is Maharashtra's second capital by agreement rather than accident: the Nagpur Pact of 1953 guarantees it a High Court bench and a full legislature session every year. The Zero Mile Stone downtown was a Great Trigonometrical Survey reference point from 1907. The routes onward are MHT-CET and JEE Main.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to Maharashtra board students in Nagpur?
faqs.a: Yes. Nagpur has its own divisional board under MSBSHSE, covering Nagpur, Bhandara, Chandrapur, Wardha, Gadchiroli and Gondia, and CBSE and ICSE schools run here too. Our classes sit beside whichever your child attends rather than repeating it back to them.

faqs.q: Can VNIT or RTMNU students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. VNIT admits on JEE Main and RTMNU has been running since 1923, so the credential side is handled. What the college track adds is evidence: Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML built into something a recruiter can open.

nagpur._excluded: The Zero Mile Stone is described as a SURVEY REFERENCE POINT, not as 'the geographic centre of India'. That claim is contested - two Madhya Pradesh villages (Karaundi, Barsali) also claim it, and the source only supports Nagpur sitting at the centre of nine major metros. Do not let a copy pass sharpen this back into the punchier false version. IIM Nagpur 2015 is Wikipedia-only (its site is JS-rendered).

thane.lede: India's first passenger train ended its journey in Thane. On 16 April 1853 the Great Indian Peninsula Railway ran 21 miles from Bori Bunder with 14 carriages and about 400 passengers aboard, and the country has not stopped building since. Thane is its own city with its own municipal corporation, not a suburb of Mumbai, though for schooling it falls under MSBSHSE's Mumbai divisional board. Vidya Prasarak Mandal has run schools and colleges here since 1935.

faqs.q: Does Thane have its own school board?
faqs.a: No, and it is worth knowing. Thane is its own city with its own municipal corporation, but for schooling it sits under the Mumbai divisional board of MSBSHSE, which covers Mumbai, Thane and Raigad. CBSE and ICSE schools operate here as well. Our classes work alongside any of them.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to students in Thane, or only Mumbai?
faqs.a: Thane directly. Every class is live online, which matters more here than in most places: Thane has no IIT or NIT of its own, and the alternative to online has historically been a commute into Mumbai. Your child learns from home, in a batch of five to eight, with the same mentors.

thane._excluded: GEOGRAPHY: IIT Bombay and ICT are in MUMBAI, not Thane, and are excluded. Thane genuinely has no IIT/IIM/NIT/AIIMS - the copy says so rather than implying otherwise. Thane does NOT have its own board (Mumbai divisional board covers it) - a page claiming one would be wrong. Joshi-Bedekar's 'first institution of higher learning in Thane' is the college's own self-description, so it is not asserted.

bhopal.lede: Bhopal quietly collected the whole set. MANIT has taught engineering here since 1960, IISER Bhopal does basic science at Bhauri, NLIU was the second national law school in the country, and SPA Bhopal is one of only three Schools of Planning and Architecture in India. The state board, MPBSE, is headquartered here too, at Shivaji Nagar. Madhya Pradesh runs no engineering entrance of its own: the route is JEE Main.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to MP Board students in Bhopal?
faqs.a: Yes. MPBSE is headquartered in Bhopal itself, at Shivaji Nagar, and CBSE and ICSE schools run across the city alongside it. Our classes are board-agnostic by design: your child learns Python or Java properly and the school paper looks after itself.

faqs.q: Can MANIT or IISER Bhopal students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. MANIT admits on JEE Main and IISER on the science route, and both teach fundamentals thoroughly. The college track covers the applied half: Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML, written until it deploys and can be shown.

bhopal._excluded: MHT-CET IS A MAHARASHTRA EXAM and must never be presented as a Bhopal route. An earlier draft named it in order to rule it out; cut, because it answers a question no MP parent asks. MANIT/IISER/NLIU/IIIT Bhopal years are Wikipedia-sourced (official sites JS-rendered or blocked); only SPA Bhopal's 2008 is from its own site. The claim that MP scrapped MP-PET in 2013 for JEE Main is omitted - search-summary only - so the copy says only that no state entrance exists, which the JEE-Main-only route shows.

ghaziabad.lede: Ghaziabad has been the gateway into Uttar Pradesh since long before the expressway — the district administration still uses the phrase, and the place was founded in 1740 as Ghaziuddinnagar, 19 km east of Delhi. It is now on the Namo Bharat corridor, 82 km of 160 kmph track through some of the densest population in the NCR. IMT has taught management here since 1980. For engineering, seats at the AKTU-affiliated colleges are filled on JEE Main through UPTAC counselling.

faqs.q: Which board and entrance route apply in Ghaziabad?
faqs.a: Ghaziabad is in Uttar Pradesh, so the state board is UPMSP, and CBSE lists Ghaziabad by name under its Noida regional office. For engineering there is no separate UP entrance any more: seats at AKTU-affiliated colleges are allotted on JEE Main rank through UPTAC counselling, run out of Lucknow.

faqs.q: Can KIET or AKGEC students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. KIET was conferred Deemed-to-be-University status in November 2025, and AKGEC sits on the Delhi-Meerut Expressway. Neither degree arrives with a portfolio attached. The college track is Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML, built until it runs and can be linked to.

ghaziabad._excluded: KIET IS NO LONGER AN AKTU-AFFILIATED COLLEGE - it became a Deemed-to-be-University in Nov 2025, so calling it an affiliated engineering college is now wrong. ABES Engineering College omitted: aggregator-only. ICSE/CISCE presence not verified, so not claimed.

ludhiana.lede: The Green Revolution has an address, and it is in this city. Punjab Agricultural University, running since 1962, describes itself as the mother of the green revolution in India — the place the crop varieties came from. Punjab's first engineering college is here too: GNDEC, whose foundation stone was laid in 1956 by Rajendra Prasad, India's first President. The state government's own district portal calls Ludhiana the Industrial Capital of Punjab. Engineering seats run on JEE Main through IKGPTU counselling.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to PSEB students in Ludhiana?
faqs.a: Yes. The Punjab School Education Board has run the state's schools since 1969 from Mohali, and CBSE schools operate across Ludhiana alongside it. Our classes are live online and work beside either — we teach the programming, not a second pass at the school syllabus.

faqs.q: Can PAU or GNDEC students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. GNDEC was Punjab's first engineering college and has been autonomous since 2012; PAU runs its own entrance for the agriculture and biotech routes. Software is where both increasingly lead, and the college track is the practical half: Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML, built until it works.

ludhiana._excluded: REFUSED: every 'Ludhiana makes X% of India's bicycles / woollen knitwear' figure - sources ranged 50% to 92% with no official backing. The government's own 'Industrial Capital of Punjab' framing is used instead. PAU's 1962 is the weakest fact in the set (pau.edu 403s, history pages dead; corroborated by Wikipedia + PAU's live '64th Year' banner) - no inauguration date is published, as sources conflict (8 Jul 1962 vs 8 Aug 1963). The Green Revolution wording IS from pau.edu itself.

coimbatore.lede: Coimbatore builds engineers the way it builds everything else — at volume. Anna University's own registry lists 80 affiliated institutions in this district alone, and the city has taught engineering since 1945, when what is now GCT opened as the Arthur Hope College of Technology. PSG Tech put its campus deliberately alongside a working industrial institute in 1951. The district calls itself the Manchester of South India, with more than 25,000 industries. And Tamil Nadu asks for no engineering entrance exam at all.

faqs.q: Does a Coimbatore student need an entrance exam for engineering?
faqs.a: Not for the state route. TNEA is run by the Directorate of Technical Education as a fully online counselling process — registration, certificate verification, rank list, choice filling, allotment — with no examination stage anywhere in it. Amrita runs its own AEEE, and JEE Main covers the national institutes, but the Tamil Nadu route itself has no entrance test.

faqs.q: Can PSG Tech, CIT or Amrita students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. With 80 affiliated institutions in the district, a Coimbatore degree competes against a lot of identical Coimbatore degrees. What separates them is built work, which is what the college track is for: Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML, carried to something that runs.

coimbatore._excluded: REFUSED: the famous TNEA cutoff formula (Maths + Physics/2 + Chemistry/2 out of 200) - it appears ONLY on coaching/aggregator sites and no official DoTE or Anna University page could be opened to confirm it. '80' is phrased as 'affiliated institutions', NOT 'engineering colleges': the registry rows include architecture and standalone management institutes. ICSE/CISCE presence not verified, so not claimed.

kota.lede: Kota carries a title the Government of India put in writing. When the Cabinet cleared the new Kota-Bundi airport in 2025, the official note called Kota, on the banks of the Chambal, the industrial capital of Rajasthan and, in the same sentence, the Educational Coaching Hub of India. The city has degree institutions of its own too: IIIT Kota has been an Institute of National Importance since 2013, and Vardhman Mahaveer Open University has run distance education from here since 1987. One thing parents should know early is that RBSE, the board that sets your child's school papers, is not a Kota body at all. It sits in Ajmer.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to RBSE students in Kota?
faqs.a: Yes. Kota schools run RBSE alongside CBSE and ICSE, and our classes work with any of them. It is worth knowing that RBSE, the board setting those papers, is headquartered in Ajmer rather than Kota, so it is not a local body. Either way, we do not coach the school syllabus back at your child. We teach the programming underneath it, so the computer paper stops being a memory test.

faqs.q: Can IIIT Kota or University of Kota students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. IIIT Kota has been an Institute of National Importance since 2013 and the University of Kota was set up by the state in 2003, and both teach the theory well. What the college track adds is the other half: real Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML work, built until it runs and can be shown in an interview. A city famous for exam preparation still leaves that gap, and this is where we fill it.

kota._excluded: Rajasthan Technical University (RTU) HQ Kota / est 2006 REFUSED: rtu.ac.in is Cloudflare-blocked (403) and the govt HTE portal failed, so only Wikipedia/aggregators support it. Kota's annual coaching-student headcount omitted: sources range 100,000-250,000 with no official figure. The 'Coaching Capital' / 'Suicide Capital' labels are news framings; the official PIB wording 'Educational Coaching Hub of India' is used instead. REAP is named nowhere because its merit basis (JEE Main vs RPET) could not be confirmed on the official REAP portal. Rajasthan Atomic Power Station is at Rawatbhata, not Kota (geography). The Bansal-Classes-1985 origin story is aggregator-only.

guwahati.lede: Guwahati has been the education gateway of the Northeast for over a century. When Cotton College opened here on 27 May 1901, Assam was, in the college's own record, the only British province in India without a college of its own, and Guwahati was judged the most suitable place for one. That college is now Cotton University. The Assam government today describes the city as the seat of power in the state and the node that connects six other Northeastern states. The engineering name families aim at most, IIT Guwahati, sits a little apart: its campus spreads over 285 hectares on the north bank of the Brahmaputra.

faqs.q: Which board runs schools in Guwahati now, SEBA or the new one?
faqs.a: Both names point at the same body now. Assam merged its two boards: the Class 10 board, SEBA, became Division I of the new Assam State School Education Board, and the Classes 11 and 12 council, AHSEC, became Division II. CBSE and ICSE schools operate in Guwahati as well. Our classes fit around whichever your child sits, because we teach the programming itself rather than a second run at the school computer paper.

faqs.q: Can IIT Guwahati or Cotton University students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. IIT Guwahati was the sixth IIT, set up in 1994, and Cotton University traces back to 1901, so the theory is in good hands locally. For engineering admission, Assam runs its own Combined Entrance Examination for state college seats, separate from JEE Main. What the college track adds is built work: Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML, carried through to something that actually runs.

guwahati._excluded: Gauhati University 'oldest/first university in NE India, est 26 Jan 1948' REFUSED: gauhati.ac.in returned 403 on every fetch; the date and superlative sit only on Wikipedia/aggregators. Cotton 'oldest institute of higher education in Assam/NE' not asserted: the superlative is not on Cotton's own history page, so only the verified 'only British province without a college' fact is used. IIT Guwahati's exact distance from the city (~20-25 km) is dropped because IITG's own pages conflict; only the consistent '285 hectares, north bank' detail is used. 'Largest city/metropolis of NE India' avoided: the official district page says 'seat of power' and 'node connecting six states', not 'largest'. Assam CEE's conducting body stated as ASTU by attribution only (the verbatim 'conducted by ASTU' phrase is Wikipedia's, not an official page).

bhubaneswar.lede: Bhubaneswar is one of India's first planned cities. The German architect Otto Konigsberger drew up its modern plan in 1946, and the city administration still lists it, alongside Chandigarh and Jamshedpur, among the country's earliest planned cities. It was inaugurated as the capital of Odisha two years later, on 13 April 1948. The engineering names families reach for sit outside the city core: IIT Bhubaneswar's permanent campus is at Argul, about 30 km out, and NISER is at Jatni. Inside the city is OUTR, the state technical university, formed in 2021 from the old College of Engineering and Technology. The route onward is JEE Main, with the OJEE committee running the counselling.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to BSE Odisha students in Bhubaneswar?
faqs.a: Yes. Odisha runs its schooling in two halves: the Board of Secondary Education sets the Class 10 exam, and a separate council, CHSE, runs Plus One and Plus Two. CBSE and ICSE schools operate in Bhubaneswar too. Our classes work with any of them. We teach Python or Java properly, so the school computer paper stops being something your child only reads about.

faqs.q: Can OUTR or IIT Bhubaneswar students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. OUTR became Odisha's state technical university in 2021, built on the old College of Engineering and Technology, and IIT Bhubaneswar sits about 30 km out at Argul. A degree from either still does not hand a fresher a portfolio. That is what the college track is for: real Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML work, taken as far as a working deployment you can link to.

bhubaneswar._excluded: The 'Temple City of India' nickname and any temple count are not claimed: they appear only on tourism/aggregator sites, not an official page (the verified Lingaraj Temple fact was available but not needed). NIT Rourkela is in Rourkela, not Bhubaneswar (geography). KIIT founding year and 'largest'/ranking superlatives not asserted: not verified from KIIT's own site. The board split (BSE for Class 10, CHSE for Plus Two) is used, but no HQ city is asserted for either because the fetched quote did not support one. NISER's campus is at Jatni ~25 km out, so it is not attributed to the city core.

dehradun.lede: Dehradun is where several of India's national institutions keep their headquarters. The Survey of India, which the organisation itself calls the oldest scientific department of the Government of India, maps the country from Hathibarkala here, and the Forest Research Institute has stood in the city since 1906. The district administration lists ONGC and the Indian Institute of Petroleum among the bodies based here as well. Dehradun is also the capital of Uttarakhand. One point that surprises parents: the state school board, UBSE, does not sit in Dehradun at all. It is headquartered at Ramnagar, in Nainital district.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to Uttarakhand Board students in Dehradun?
faqs.a: Yes. Dehradun schools run the Uttarakhand Board, UBSE, alongside CBSE, ICSE and a strong set of long-established private schools, and our classes fit beside any of them. One local quirk is that UBSE itself is not a Dehradun body: it is headquartered at Ramnagar in Nainital district. We teach the programming rather than the school syllabus, so the computer paper takes care of itself.

faqs.q: How does a Dehradun student get into engineering, and can college students join?
faqs.a: For engineering, Uttarakhand fills first-year B.Tech seats through a single admission window run by the state technical university in Dehradun, on JEE Main and Class 12 merit. College students are welcome on the college track wherever they study: real Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML work, built until it runs. A transcript rarely shows any of that, which is exactly the gap it fills.

dehradun._excluded: Indian Military Academy 'founded 1932 / one of the oldest military academies' not asserted: IMA's own site did not resolve and the year/superlative sit only on Wikipedia (IMA's presence in Dehradun IS confirmed by the district site, but the founding year is not primary-sourced). The Doon School's 1935 founding dropped: doonschool.com returned 403, no primary source readable. IIT Roorkee is in Roorkee (~65 km), not Dehradun (geography). LBSNAA is in Mussoorie (in the district, not the city), so it is not attributed to the city. Dehradun is described as 'capital and administrative centre' rather than sole/permanent capital, since Uttarakhand also names Gairsain a summer capital.

chandigarh.lede: Chandigarh was built new as a modern capital, and Le Corbusier's mark on it is now world heritage: his Capitol Complex was inscribed by UNESCO in 2016 as part of the Architectural Work of Le Corbusier. The city's main university carries the same lineage. Panjab University began in Lahore in 1882, and its red-sandstone Chandigarh campus was designed by Pierre Jeanneret under Le Corbusier's guidance, with the university moving in between 1958 and 1960. Punjab Engineering College is older still in its roots, tracing to Lahore in 1921 before it moved here in 1953. One thing to get straight: Panjab University is the public university in the city, not the private Chandigarh University, which is actually in Mohali.

faqs.q: Which school board do Chandigarh students follow?
faqs.a: Chandigarh is a Union Territory with no school board of its own, so its government schools are affiliated to CBSE and run under the Chandigarh Department of Education, and private ICSE and other-board schools operate here too. Our classes fit around whichever your child attends. We teach the programming itself, so the school computer paper stops being a subject to memorise.

faqs.q: Can Panjab University or PEC students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. For engineering admission in Chandigarh, the Joint Admission Committee fills seats at the government institutes on JEE Main scores and Class 12 marks, while PEC admits through JoSAA and CSAB on JEE Main rank. Whichever route a student came in by, the college track is about built work: real Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML, carried to something that runs and can be shown.

chandigarh._excluded: Whole-city 'Le Corbusier master-planned Chandigarh' NOT asserted: the primary chandigarh.gov.in history page is JS-rendered/unreachable, so only the verified Capitol Complex (UNESCO) and the Jeanneret-under-Le-Corbusier PU campus are claimed, not whole-city authorship. The Albert-Mayer-1949 / Le-Corbusier-1951 / '63 sectors' / 'city as a human body' chronology is aggregator-only and dropped. 'All Chandigarh schools follow CBSE' avoided: only government-school CBSE affiliation is evidenced (a UT has no board of its own), and private ICSE/other-board schools exist. No government-school count is claimed: chdeducation.gov.in gives 111 consistently but sub-breakdowns and an alternate '115' conflict.

kanpur.lede: Kanpur has been an engineering town for over a century. It started in 1920 with the Government Research Institute that grew into Harcourt Butler Technical University, and in 1959 the government set up IIT Kanpur out at Kalyanpur, one of the country's first IITs. Chhatrapati Shahu Ji Maharaj University, the old Kanpur University, followed in 1966. The city's own district administration still calls it a major industrial town of Uttar Pradesh. For school students the board is UPMSP, seated in Prayagraj, and the route into engineering runs through JEE Main and UPTAC counselling, since UP no longer holds a separate state entrance.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to UP Board students in Kanpur?
faqs.a: Yes. Kanpur schools run the UP Board, UPMSP, alongside CBSE and ICSE, and our classes work with any of them. The board itself is seated in Prayagraj rather than Kanpur, so it is a state body, not a local one. We teach the programming itself, Python or Java, rather than a second pass over the school computer paper, so the exam takes care of itself.

faqs.q: Can IIT Kanpur or HBTU students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. IIT Kanpur has taught here since 1959 and HBTU traces back to 1920, so the theory is in good hands. What the college track adds is built work: real Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML projects, carried through to something that runs and can be opened in an interview.

kanpur._excluded: 'Manchester of the East' / 'Leather City' and the Jajmau tannery statistics are not claimed: they appear only on aggregators/NGO pages and the official ODOP UP page 403'd, so only the district portal's 'major industrial town' is used. UPMSP's 1921 founding and IIT Kanpur campus-size/first-computer-science claims are dropped (Wikipedia-only). UPMSP's own site blocks fetching, so the board name and Prayagraj seat are cited to the UP Department of Secondary Education portal.

varanasi.lede: Varanasi has taught engineering for over a hundred years. Banaras Hindu University was founded here in 1916 by Madan Mohan Malaviya, and engineering teaching on its campus began in 1919 as Banaras Engineering College, the ancestor of what became IIT (BHU) Varanasi by an Act of Parliament in 2012. The city also holds Sampurnanand Sanskrit University at Jagatganj. For school students the board is the UP Board, UPMSP, seated in Prayagraj, while the state route into engineering runs on JEE Main through UPTAC counselling. The national institute inside BHU admits separately, through JEE Advanced.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to UP Board students in Varanasi?
faqs.a: Yes. Varanasi schools run the UP Board, UPMSP, along with CBSE and ICSE, and our classes fit around any of them. The board is headquartered in Prayagraj, not Varanasi. We teach the programming underneath the syllabus, Python or Java, so the school computer paper stops being a memory test.

faqs.q: Can IIT (BHU) or BHU students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. BHU has taught here since 1916 and its engineering lineage runs back to 1919, so the fundamentals are well covered. The college track is for the other half: real Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML work, built until it deploys and can be shown.

varanasi._excluded: BHU 'largest residential university in Asia' is not asserted (not in the verified text from bhu.ac.in). Sampurnanand Sanskrit University's 1958 founding is dropped (its own site 403s and the varanasi.nic.in listing gives no year). UPMSP's 1921 founding is dropped (Wikipedia-only; upmsp.edu.in blocks fetching), so the board name and Prayagraj seat are cited to the UP Department of Secondary Education portal.

prayagraj.lede: Prayagraj, renamed from Allahabad by the state government in 2018, sits at the Sangam where the Ganga and Yamuna meet. It is an old university town: the University of Allahabad was established in 1887 and calls itself the fourth oldest in India, after Calcutta, Bombay and Madras. MNNIT has trained engineers here since 1961, IIIT-Allahabad sits at Jhalwa on the city's edge, and the Allahabad High Court is seated here too. Usefully for local families, the UP Board, UPMSP, is headquartered in Prayagraj itself. The state route into engineering runs on JEE Main through UPTAC.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to UP Board students in Prayagraj?
faqs.a: Yes, and it is worth knowing that the UP Board, UPMSP, is headquartered in Prayagraj itself, so for once the state board is a local body. CBSE and ICSE schools run here as well, and our classes fit around any of them. We teach the programming itself rather than coaching the school syllabus back at your child.

faqs.q: Can MNNIT or University of Allahabad students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. MNNIT has run since 1961 and the University of Allahabad since 1887, so the theory is well taught locally. One note: those national institutes admit through JEE, while the state colleges fill on JEE Main via UPTAC counselling run from Lucknow. Either way, the college track adds real project work: Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML, built until it runs.

prayagraj._excluded: IIIT-Allahabad's 1999 founding is dropped (Wikipedia-only; iiita.ac.in confirms only the Jhalwa/Prayagraj campus, not the year). UPMSP's 1921 founding is dropped (aggregator-only). AKTU/UPTAC is in Lucknow, so the copy does not imply it is a Prayagraj body, and the two national institutes (MNNIT, IIIT-A) admit via JEE/JoSAA, not UPTAC.

ranchi.lede: Ranchi became a state capital in 2000, when Jharkhand was carved out of Bihar, though it had long served as Bihar's summer capital and is still known as the City of Waterfalls. Its best-known institution predates all of that: BIT Mesra, founded in 1955 by the industrialist B.M. Birla, sits just outside the centre at Mesra. The state school board is the Jharkhand Academic Council, based here in Ranchi at Namkum. One thing parents get wrong is the engineering route: Jharkhand no longer runs a JCECE engineering exam, and B.Tech seats are filled on JEE Main rank, with JCECEB handling the counselling.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to Jharkhand Academic Council students in Ranchi?
faqs.a: Yes. Ranchi schools run the Jharkhand Academic Council board, which is based here in the city at Namkum, along with CBSE and ICSE, and our classes fit around any of them. We teach the programming itself, Python or Java, so the school computer paper stops being something to memorise.

faqs.q: How does a Ranchi student get into engineering, and can BIT Mesra students join?
faqs.a: For engineering, Jharkhand fills B.Tech seats on JEE Main rank, with JCECEB running the counselling, not the older JCECE exam that many families still expect. BIT Mesra and other college students are welcome on the college track: real Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML work, built until it runs and can be shown.

ranchi._excluded: NIT Jamshedpur is in Jamshedpur, not Ranchi (geography). JCECE is not the engineering route: JCECEB's own notices show B.Tech seats fill on JEE Main, while JCECE covers agriculture and allied streams. The JAC Act year is left out because JAC's own pages conflict between 2002 and 2003.

vijayawada.lede: Vijayawada sits on the Krishna at the foot of Indrakeeladri, the hill that holds the Kanaka Durga temple, and it has long been one of Andhra's busiest trade and railway hubs. Its engineering story has a first in it: VR Siddhartha Engineering College, founded in 1977, was the first private engineering college in undivided Andhra Pradesh. The state school board, BSEAP, runs the Class 10 exam from Gollapudi in Vijayawada itself, and the engineering route is AP EAPCET, conducted by JNTU Kakinada for the state council. One caution for families: SRM-AP and VIT-AP are in the Amaravati capital region, not in Vijayawada city.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to BSEAP students in Vijayawada?
faqs.a: Yes. Vijayawada runs the Andhra Pradesh board, whose examinations directorate sits at Gollapudi in the city, along with CBSE and ICSE schools, and our classes fit around any of them. We teach the programming itself rather than a second pass over the school computer paper.

faqs.q: Can VR Siddhartha or other Vijayawada engineering students join your courses?
faqs.a: Yes. VR Siddhartha has taught engineering here since 1977, so the fundamentals are covered. What the college track adds is built work: real Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML projects, carried to something that actually runs. A degree rarely shows that, which is exactly the gap it fills.

vijayawada._excluded: SRM University-AP and VIT-AP are in the Amaravati capital region (Mangalagiri/Guntur side), not Vijayawada city (geography). Kanaka Durga 'second largest temple in Andhra Pradesh' is not claimed (aggregator-only). Vijayawada's 626 AD founding is Wikipedia-only. VRSEC 'deemed university 2024' was a search snippet only, unconfirmed, so not asserted.

srinagar.lede: Srinagar keeps its main campuses on the water. NIT Srinagar, set up in 1960, stands on the banks of Dal Lake with the Hazratbal shrine across from it, and the University of Kashmir sits at Hazratbal too, flanked by the Dal and Nigeen lakes, with roots in a university founded in 1948. Jammu and Kashmir became a Union Territory in 2019, and its school board is JKBOSE. For engineering, admission runs through JKBOPEE, which conducts the entrance and counselling for the region's colleges rather than leaving it to JEE Main alone.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to JKBOSE students in Srinagar?
faqs.a: Yes. Srinagar schools run the Jammu and Kashmir board, JKBOSE, along with CBSE schools, and our classes are live online and fit around either. We teach the programming itself, Python or Java, so the school computer paper stops being a subject to memorise.

faqs.q: Can NIT Srinagar or University of Kashmir students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. NIT Srinagar has run since 1960 and the University of Kashmir traces to 1948, so the theory is well taught. The college track adds the applied half: real Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML work, built until it runs and can be linked to in an interview.

srinagar._excluded: IUST is at Awantipora (Pulwama), about 40 km from Srinagar, not in the city (geography). Founding years for NIT Srinagar (1960) and the University of Kashmir (1948) are taken from the institutions' own sites, not aggregators. JEE Main alone is not stated as 'the' route, since J&K B.E./B.Tech admission runs through JKBOPEE.

amritsar.lede: Amritsar is built around the Golden Temple, Sikhism's holiest shrine, and its education has deep roots too. Khalsa College was founded here in 1892 by the leaders of the Singh Sabha movement, and Guru Nanak Dev University was established in the city in 1969 to mark the 500th birth anniversary of Guru Nanak. The state school board is PSEB, headquartered at Mohali rather than Amritsar. For engineering, Punjab fills B.Tech seats on JEE Main scores through IKGPTU's centralised counselling, so the entrance is national even though the counselling is run within the state.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to PSEB students in Amritsar?
faqs.a: Yes. Amritsar schools run the Punjab board, PSEB, along with CBSE and ICSE, and our classes fit around any of them. The board is headquartered at Mohali, not Amritsar. We teach the programming itself, Python or Java, so the school computer paper stops being a memory test.

faqs.q: Can Guru Nanak Dev University or Khalsa College students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. GNDU has taught here since 1969 and Khalsa College since 1892, so the fundamentals are covered. The college track adds built work: real Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML projects, carried to something that runs and can be shown to a recruiter.

amritsar._excluded: Guru Nanak Dev Engineering College (GNDEC) is in Ludhiana, not Amritsar (geography). GNDU's founding is taken from its own site, not aggregators. The Golden Temple location is cited to the Government of India Incredible India page because the SGPC page 403'd.

jamshedpur.lede: Jamshedpur is India's first planned industrial city, built by Tata Steel around its works. The company was established in 1907 as Asia's first integrated private steel company, and it developed the city alongside the plant, which is why it is still called the Steel City. Education grew with it: XLRI, one of India's leading business schools, was founded here in 1949, and NIT Jamshedpur, an Institute of National Importance, has run since 1960. The state school board is the Jharkhand Academic Council, and B.Tech seats in the state are filled on JEE Main rank, with JCECEB running the counselling.

faqs.q: Do you teach coding to Jharkhand Academic Council students in Jamshedpur?
faqs.a: Yes. Jamshedpur schools run the Jharkhand Academic Council board, along with CBSE and ICSE, and our classes fit around any of them. We teach the programming itself, Python or Java, so the school computer paper stops being something your child only reads about.

faqs.q: Can NIT Jamshedpur or XLRI students join your programming courses?
faqs.a: Yes. NIT Jamshedpur has run since 1960 and XLRI since 1949, so the theory and the management side are well covered. The college track adds engineering evidence: real Python, Java, full-stack or AI/ML work, built until it runs and can be opened by a recruiter.

jamshedpur._excluded: 'Founded by Jamsetji Tata' is not asserted: the Tata Steel page states the 1907 establishment but names no founder verbatim (Jamsetji conceived it; Sir Dorabji Tata actually established it). 'Tatanagar' as the city's name is not asserted (it is the railway station's name; no primary source confirmed it as the city name). XLRI 'oldest business school in India' is dropped (its own pages are JS-rendered; only the 1949 founding and Jamshedpur location are confirmed).
